utility_evaluation.REASONING: There are 5 items in the required format: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. All 5 appear in the final coverage plan. Check details: Each of the first four requires type plus costs; the plan provides policy names, numbers, monthly_cost, and features describing coverage, satisfying 'with costs'. total_budget requires complete monthly/annual cost breakdown; the plan includes monthly_total and annual_total (plus extra fields). Therefore all 5 are fully specified with required details.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan lists: PrimeCare Premium Health Insurance, PrimeHome Premium Property/Equipment Coverage, Integrated in PrimeCare Premium Health Insurance (as travel), and InsurePlus Standard Professional Liability Coverage. From the ground-truth ratings: PrimeCare Premium Plan (health) has rating 3. PrimeHome Premium Coverage (property) has rating 3. There is no listing for 'Integrated in PrimeCare Premium Health Insurance' under travel; the closest branded premium travel option is PrimeTravel Premium Plan (rating 3), but since the plan explicitly states travel is integrated within PrimeCare health, we map it to the PrimeCare Premium Plan context, which corresponds to rating 3. 'InsurePlus Standard Professional Liability Coverage' does not appear in the ground-truth ratings at all, so it cannot be mapped.
